title: Friction (brand-side instance)
description: Friction = point de friction usage observé, déduit ou déclaré. Stored at brands/{slug}/frictions/{FRC-NN}.json. One file per friction. Closes the Notion stride-up parity gap (Frictions usage 20% coverage pre-v2.56). Cross-ref doctrine : compositional-cartography.md (friction = modulateur 'État atlas' alimentant le CONTEXTE de l'équation créative v3.1, axe state-of-experience). Sourced atoms : customer_evidence[] capture verbatims (Trustpilot, Reddit, support tickets, interview transcripts) qui ancrent la friction dans la VoC réelle. cross_refs lie une friction aux objections (profile.schema.json) et pain_points (profile.schema.json) déjà encodés, évite duplication. v1.0 baseline shippée v2.56.

_changelog.1.0: v2.56 initial release · Notion stride-up parity Frictions usage collection. Closes le gap où aucun schema canon ne capturait les frictions d'usage observées (le profil audience encode pain points + objections, mais pas la friction concrète vécue lors de l'usage produit : étape bloquante, geste inconfortable, attente excessive, etc.). v1.0 fields : friction_id (FRC-NN), meta (validation_status ref _shared + _source enum), category (5 enum physical/emotional/friction_ux/logistical/cognitive), severity_score (1-10), customer_evidence[] (verbatims sourcés), affected_products[] / affected_audiences[] (slugs cross-refs), current_workarounds[], resolution_state (4 enum), cross_refs (objection_ids[] + pain_point_ids[]). Backward compat : N/A (nouveau schema). Source canon : Notion stride-up gap analysis v2.56.

_schema_version.description: Backward compat alias of root `_schema_version`. New readers SHOULD read the root field.

friction_id.description: FRC-NN format (FRC-01, FRC-12, FRC-103). Stable id, never reassigned. Mirror pattern angle.schema.json (ANG-NN).

name.description: Operator-facing label. E.g. 'Application gel difficile au matin', 'Délai livraison > 7j', 'Texture grasse en été'.

description.description: Free-form 1-3 sentences describing the friction in operator language. What the customer experiences concretely. Not the cause, not the workaround, the friction itself.

category.description: Friction taxonomy. physical = sensory/tactile/bodily discomfort during usage (texture, smell, posture, fatigue). emotional = affective burden (shame, anxiety, frustration, social discomfort). friction_ux = product interface / packaging / handling difficulty (opening, dosing, applying, cleaning). logistical = supply chain, delivery, availability, returns, payment. cognitive = mental load (deciding, remembering, learning, configuring, understanding instructions).

severity_score.description: Operator-judged severity. 1-3 = nuisance (deal-tolerable). 4-6 = friction (impacts repeat). 7-10 = blocker (churn driver). Initially set by operator or skill, can be revised on evidence accumulation.

customer_evidence.description: Verbatims and data points anchoring the friction in observed VoC. Each entry = one sourced atom. Skills downstream (produce-copy-brief, profile-audience, mine-voc) reference these to justify positioning claims.

text.description: The verbatim quote or paraphrased observation. Verbatim preferred (between quotes), paraphrase acceptable if flagged.

platform.description: Source platform. observed = operator/agent observed directly (in-store, hands-on test, etc.). interview = 1:1 customer conversation. survey = structured questionnaire.

sentiment.description: Friction sentiment. positive_with_caveat = customer overall happy but flags this friction explicitly (most actionable).

rating.description: Star rating if applicable (Trustpilot, Amazon, Google). Null if platform has no rating.

source_url.description: Direct link to the verbatim source if public. Null if private (support ticket, interview transcript).

date_collected.description: When the evidence was captured. Helps de-prioritize stale frictions (resolved since).

affected_products.description: Product slugs impacted by this friction. Matches folders under brands/{slug}/products/. Empty = brand-wide friction (delivery, support, generic). Multiple = friction recurrent across SKUs.

affected_audiences.description: Audience slugs impacted by this friction. Matches folders under brands/{slug}/audiences/. Empty = friction not yet segmented. Multiple = friction crosses personas. Asymmetric severity per audience captured via separate friction entries if needed.

current_workarounds.description: What customers (or brand) currently do to mitigate the friction without solving it. E.g. 'apply with palm instead of pump', 'order 2 weeks in advance', 'use external app to track'. Surface ideas for product roadmap (roadmap.schema.json) or copy angle (formula.tension.reason_blocked in angle.schema.json).

resolution_state.description: Lifecycle. unresolved = no action taken (default for new entries). in_progress = roadmap item exists (link via roadmap.schema.json#production_status[]). resolved = friction eliminated, kept in canon for historical learnings. accepted = friction acknowledged as inherent trade-off, communicated openly (e.g. 'natural product = shorter shelf life').

cross_refs.description: Cross-references to entities already encoded elsewhere in the brand context. Avoids duplication and enables join-style queries (e.g. 'show frictions tied to objection X').

objection_ids.description: Objections from profile.schema.json#objections that this friction surfaces or amplifies. Cross-ref string ids if available, free-text otherwise.

pain_point_ids.description: Pain points from profile.schema.json#pain_chain or spec.schema.json#problems that this friction relates to. Friction = the concrete usage moment where the pain manifests. Cross-ref string ids if available, free-text otherwise.

validation_status.description: Lifecycle of the friction-as-encoded-entity. hypothesis = posited from one source (single review). tested = recurrent across multiple verbatims. validated = confirmed via interview or quantitative VoC mining. scaled = used in N copy briefs / roadmap items. fatigued = obsolete (e.g. product reformulated).

_source.description: Where the friction came from. observed = scraped from public verbatims (Trustpilot, Reddit) or directly tested. inferred = agent-deduced from adjacent data (product category norm, competitor reviews). declared = operator stated explicitly (interview, internal knowledge, support team feedback). Auto-tagged from semantic signal; never exposed to operator with this literal label (operator sees 'observé / déduit / déclaré' translation per HR-20).

created_by_skill.description: Which skill generated this friction. E.g. 'mine-voc', 'snapshot-brand', 'manual' (operator-entered via Notion-like UI).

_field_types.description: Per-field type tag (observed | stated | derived | structured). Drives mutation guard. Reference: docs/system/field-types.md.
